---
title: "quiz11"
title-block-style: none
format: live-html
engine: knitr
sidebar:
  style: docked
# resources:
---

{{< include /_extensions/r-wasm/live/_knitr.qmd >}}
{{< include /_extensions/r-wasm/live/_gradethis.qmd >}}

<script>
document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", () => {
  const params = new URLSearchParams(window.location.search);
  const showQ = params.get("q");

  // Show only the requested section
  if (showQ) {
    document.querySelectorAll("section[id]").forEach(section => {
      if (section.id !== showQ) section.style.display = "none";
    });
  }

  // Hide page title
  document.querySelectorAll(".quarto-title-block")
    .forEach(el => el.style.display = "none");

  // Hide ALL section titles (## ...)
  document.querySelectorAll("section[id] > h2")
    .forEach(h => h.style.display = "none");
});
</script>

<!-- questions 1 to 4 are on quercus directly. theyre related to healthcare and user engagement on websites -->

## Q5 — Power (Right-tailed) {q5a}

A company wants to test whether a new training program increases the average productivity score.

Suppose the population standard deviation is $\sigma = 10$ and the sample size is $n = 25$.

We test:

$$
H_0: \mu = 70 \quad \text{vs.} \quad H_a: \mu > 70
$$

at significance level $\alpha = 0.05$.

Assume the true mean is 75.

What is the power of the test?
Round your answer to 4 decimal places.

```{webr}
mu0 <- ___
mu_true <- ___
sigma <- ___
n <- ___
alpha <- ___

z_alpha <- qnorm(1 - alpha)

cutoff <- ___ + z_alpha * ___ / ___

z <- (cutoff - ___) / (___ / ___)

1 - pnorm(z)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
mu0 <- 70
mu_true <- 75
sigma <- 10
n <- 25
alpha <- 0.05

z_alpha <- qnorm(1 - alpha)

cutoff <- mu0 + z_alpha * sigma / sqrt(n)

z <- (cutoff - mu_true) / (sigma / sqrt(n))

1 - pnorm(z)
# 0.8038
```

## Q5 — Power (Right-tailed) {q5b}

A company wants to test whether a new training program increases the average productivity score.

Suppose the population standard deviation is $\sigma = 13$ and the sample size is $n = 49$.

We test:

$$
H_0: \mu = 68 \quad \text{vs.} \quad H_a: \mu > 68
$$

at significance level $\alpha = 0.05$.

Assume the true mean is 73.

What is the power of the test?
Round your answer to 4 decimal places.

```{webr}
mu0 <- ___
mu_true <- ___
sigma <- ___
n <- ___
alpha <- ___

z_alpha <- qnorm(1 - alpha)

cutoff <- ___ + z_alpha * ___ / ___

z <- (cutoff - ___) / (___ / ___)

1 - pnorm(z)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
mu0 <- 68
mu_true <- 73
sigma <- 13
n <- 49
alpha <- 0.05

z_alpha <- qnorm(1 - alpha)

cutoff <- mu0 + z_alpha * sigma / sqrt(n)

z <- (cutoff - mu_true) / (sigma / sqrt(n))

1 - pnorm(z)
# 0.8526
```

## Q6 — Power (Left-tailed) {q6a}

A manufacturer wants to test whether a new process decreases the average number of defects.

Suppose the population standard deviation is $\sigma = 8$ and the sample size is $n = 16$.

We test:

$$
H_0: \mu = 50 \quad \text{vs.} \quad H_a: \mu < 50
$$

at significance level $\alpha = 0.05$.

Assume the true mean is 46.

What is the power of the test?
Round your answer to 4 decimal places.

```{webr}
mu0 <- ___
mu_true <- ___
sigma <- ___
n <- ___
alpha <- ___

z_alpha <- qnorm(alpha)

cutoff <- ___ + z_alpha * ___ / ___

z <- (cutoff - ___) / (___ / ___)

pnorm(z)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
mu0 <- 50
mu_true <- 46
sigma <- 8
n <- 16
alpha <- 0.05

z_alpha <- qnorm(alpha)

cutoff <- mu0 + z_alpha * sigma / sqrt(n)

z <- (cutoff - mu_true) / (sigma / sqrt(n))

pnorm(z)
# 0.6388
```
## Q6 — Power (Left-tailed) {q6b}

A manufacturer wants to test whether a new process decreases the average number of defects.

Suppose the population standard deviation is $\sigma = 10$ and the sample size is $n = 36$.

We test:

$$
H_0: \mu = 54 \quad \text{vs.} \quad H_a: \mu < 54
$$

at significance level $\alpha = 0.05$.

Assume the true mean is 50.

What is the power of the test?
Round your answer to 4 decimal places.

```{webr}
mu0 <- ___
mu_true <- ___
sigma <- ___
n <- ___
alpha <- ___

z_alpha <- qnorm(alpha)

cutoff <- ___ + z_alpha * ___ / ___

z <- (cutoff - ___) / (___ / ___)

pnorm(z)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
mu0 <- 54
mu_true <- 50
sigma <- 10
n <- 36
alpha <- 0.05

z_alpha <- qnorm(alpha)

cutoff <- mu0 + z_alpha * sigma / sqrt(n)

z <- (cutoff - mu_true) / (sigma / sqrt(n))

pnorm(z)
# 0.7749
```
## Q7 — Type II Error ($\beta$) {q7a}

A school tests whether a new tutoring method increases average exam scores.

Suppose the population standard deviation is $\sigma = 12$ and the sample size is $n = 36$.

We test:

$$
H_0: \mu = 75 \quad \text{vs.} \quad H_a: \mu > 75
$$

at significance level $\alpha = 0.05$.

Assume the true mean is 79.

What is the probability of a Type II error ($\beta$)?
Round your answer to 4 decimal places.

```{webr}
mu0 <- ___
mu_true <- ___
sigma <- ___
n <- ___
alpha <- ___

z_alpha <- qnorm(1 - alpha)

cutoff <- ___ + z_alpha * ___ / ___

z <- (cutoff - ___) / (___ / ___)

pnorm(z)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
mu0 <- 75
mu_true <- 79
sigma <- 12
n <- 36
alpha <- 0.05

z_alpha <- qnorm(1 - alpha)

cutoff <- mu0 + z_alpha * sigma / sqrt(n)

z <- (cutoff - mu_true) / (sigma / sqrt(n))

pnorm(z)
# 0.3612
```

## Q7 — Type II Error ($\beta$) {q7b}

A school tests whether a new tutoring method increases average exam scores.

Suppose the population standard deviation is $\sigma = 14$ and the sample size is $n = 49$.

We test:

$$
H_0: \mu = 71 \quad \text{vs.} \quad H_a: \mu > 71
$$

at significance level $\alpha = 0.05$.

Assume the true mean is 76.

What is the probability of a Type II error ($\beta$)?
Round your answer to 4 decimal places.

```{webr}
mu0 <- ___
mu_true <- ___
sigma <- ___
n <- ___
alpha <- ___

z_alpha <- qnorm(1 - alpha)

cutoff <- ___ + z_alpha * ___ / ___

z <- (cutoff - ___) / (___ / ___)

pnorm(z)
```

```{webr}
#| echo: false
#| output: false
mu0 <- 71
mu_true <- 76
sigma <- 14
n <- 49
alpha <- 0.05

z_alpha <- qnorm(1 - alpha)

cutoff <- mu0 + z_alpha * sigma / sqrt(n)

z <- (cutoff - mu_true) / (sigma / sqrt(n))

pnorm(z)
# 0.1962
```
